title: Protecting sea lanes through partnership: exercise Bell Buoy concludes in Auckland
content: Exercise Bell Buoy concluded in early May 2026 after two weeks of intense operational activity in Auckland, New Zealand, bringing together international military and maritime partners to strengthen cooperation in an increasingly complex global security environment.

title: Commission reinforces resilience of money market funds with new guidance
content: Today’s report provides, alongside the FAQs, key additional guidance and aims to support more consistent and well-calibrated supervision of MMFs across the EU, strengthening the resilience of the sector.

title: EU coordinates international support for the recovery of Syria's economy and reconstruction process
content: The European Commission, together with the Syrian Transitional Authorities, hosted today the Syria Partnership Coordination Forum in Brussels. This meeting further delivered on the main commitments of the Forum

title: New law strengthening water protection in EU enters into force
content: The new regulations will make Europe more water-resilient, while boosting its zero-pollution and water-resilience ambitions.
snippet: The new regulations will make Europe more water-resilient, while boosting its zero-pollution and water-resilience ambitions.

title: Commission approves €1.5 billion Spanish State aid scheme to support farmers affected by 2025-2026 floods in Andalusia and Extremadura
content: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €1.5 billion Spanish scheme to support farmers affected by a series of adverse meteorological events in the regions of Andalusia and Extremadura between 10 November 2025 and 9 February 2026.

title: Protecting passengers and helping EU transport navigate the Middle East crisis
content: Commission guidance for the transport and tourism sector, which clarifies how existing EU rules can help deal with the impacts of the conflict in the Middle East.
snippet: Commission guidance for the transport and tourism sector, which clarifies how existing EU rules can help deal with the impacts of the conflict in the Middle East.

title: From Orbit to the Water Tap: 11th CASSINI Hackathon
content: From 24 to 26 April 2026, the 11th CASSINI Hackathon: Space for Water took place simultaneously across ten countries, bringing together 722 participants creating 145 teams to apply EU Space Programme technologies to one of the most pressing challenges of our time: water.

title: Europe Day: celebrating unity, peace, and shared progress
content: On 9 May, we celebrate Europe Day. It marks the anniversary of the Schuman Declaration, in which French Foreign Minister Robert Schuman proposed the creation of a European Coal and Steel Community. This proposal marked the first step towards European integration.

title: Commission reports on progress in implementing Pact on Migration and Asylum
content: On 8 May 2026, the Commission published a report on the state of play on the Pact on Migration and Asylum's implementation, showing that Member States have significantly advanced in implementing the Pact. However, continuous continuous efforts are needed for the Pact's full entry into application.

title: Summary report: Reality check on simplifying excise procedures
content: A reality check on simplifying excise procedures took place on 23 March.

title: EU prepares coordinated response to address the jet fuel supply situation as the conflict in the Middle East persists
content: Today’s Oil Coordination Group meeting gathered experts from the European Commission, EU countries, and the Energy Community secretariat to discuss the oil security of supply situation in Europe and how to best coordinate at EU level as the Middle East conflict continues.

title: EU, Brazil and China launch open coalition to boost integrity and effectiveness of carbon markets
content: The EU, Brazil and China have launched a new international coalition to strengthen the integrity, transparency and effectiveness of carbon markets worldwide, boosting global cooperation on carbon pricing and reinforcing support for the Paris Agreement and climate neutrality.

title: European Master's in Translation: winners announced for 'Your thesis in 3 minutes' contest
content: 10 European Master's in Translation students competed in a live online final to present their master's thesis in 3 minutes. Meet the 4 winners of the 2026 contest.
snippet: 10 European Master's in Translation students competed in a live online final to present their master's thesis in 3 minutes. Meet the 4 winners of the 2026 contest.

title: The EU boosts biotech leadership through breakthrough Parkinson’s gene therapy in Poland
content: A new STEP story highlights how EU support is helping advance an innovative gene therapy for Parkinson’s disease at Mazowiecki Szpital Bródnowski in Warsaw.

title: Fighting housing exclusion and supporting independent living: the first-ever EU Anti-Poverty Strategy presented
content: The European Commission today unveiled the first-ever EU Anti-Poverty Strategy, together with a proposal for a Council Recommendation on fighting housing exclusion and reinforced action under the Strategy for the Rights of Persons with Disabilities.

title: Commission and European Central Bank host annual conference on European financial integration
content: The conference marks the launch of the Commission's annual European Financial Stability and Integration Review (EFSIR).
snippet: The conference marks the launch of the Commission's annual European Financial Stability and Integration Review (EFSIR).

title: Commission seeks feedback on revised sustainability reporting standards
content: Stakeholders are invited to provide feedback within one month via the Have Your Say portal.

title: The real reason people stay at their jobs
content: Is it just better wages and perks, or something more? Find out the main reasons why employees stay loyal to companies.

title: Commission invites comments on draft revised State aid rules for air transport
content: The European Commission has today launched a public consultation inviting all interested parties to comment on its draft revised Guidelines on State aid to the air transport sector. These will replace the existing Guidelines, which were adopted in 2014.

title: Your skills are key to a brighter AI future
content: AI could be a force for good in our careers and lives. It could also lead us down a darker path. Read about three such futures that could become reality, all depending on the action Europe takes now.
snippet: AI could be a force for good in our careers and lives. It could also lead us down a darker path. Read about three such futures that could become reality, all depending on the action Europe takes now.

title: European Youth Week highlights housing challenge for the next generation
content: Housing was amongst the topics discussed during the European Youth Week in late-April, where young people shared their experiences with the Commission and several other institutions.
snippet: Housing was amongst the topics discussed during the European Youth Week in late-April, where young people shared their experiences with the Commission and several other institutions.

title: Commission unconditionally approves Suzano's acquisition of Kimberly-Clark IFP
content: The European Commission has unconditionally approved, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition by Suzano S.A. ('Suzano') of Kimberly Clark IFP NewCo B.V. ('Kimberly-Clark IFP').
snippet: The European Commission has unconditionally approved, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition by Suzano S.A. ('Suzano') of Kimberly Clark IFP NewCo B.V. ('Kimberly-Clark IFP').

title: EU and Syria meet to take their relations and cooperation forward
content: On Monday, 11 May, the European Union and the Syrian Transitional Authorities will convene two high-level meetings in Brussels to reinforce EU-Syria relations and discuss ways to support Syria's socio-economic recovery, reconstruction, and long-term stability.

title: Call for proposals: strengthening the European Child Guarantee and supporting Roma NEETs
content: The European Social Fund Plus (ESF+) has launched a new call for proposals to combat child poverty and help Roma young people access the labour market. Applications are open until 30 September 2026.
snippet: The European Social Fund Plus (ESF+) has launched a new call for proposals to combat child poverty and help Roma young people access the labour market. Applications are open until 30 September 2026.

title: Unlock Europe’s bioeconomy potential: Join the new Investment Deployment Group
content: The Commission is looking for experts to join the European Bioeconomy Investment Deployment Group, a new platform designed to accelerate funding for sustainable, bio-based innovation.
snippet: The Commission is looking for experts to join the European Bioeconomy Investment Deployment Group, a new platform designed to accelerate funding for sustainable, bio-based innovation.

title: Side by Side: Testimonials of EU and non-EU citizens working together
content: EU businesses face labour shortages across many sectors. Workers from outside the EU are helping to fill this gap. Side by Side video series show how non-EU workers contribute their skills to EU businesses, helping address labour shortages through successful training and integration.

title: Commission publishes guidance to support EU transport sector affected by the Middle East crisis
content: The Middle East crisis continues to affect Europe’s transport and tourism sector, notably through disruptions to fuel supply and the closure of certain air and shipping routes.
snippet: The Middle East crisis continues to affect Europe’s transport and tourism sector, notably through disruptions to fuel supply and the closure of certain air and shipping routes.

title: Commission approves €5 billion German State aid scheme to support decarbonisation of industry
content: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €5 billion German scheme to help companies in industrial sectors decarbonise their production processes.
snippet: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €5 billion German scheme to help companies in industrial sectors decarbonise their production processes.

title: Top scientists to identify future members of European Research Council’s Scientific Council
content: The six members of the Identification Committee will oversee the renewal of the Scientific Council for Horizon Europe 2028–2034.
snippet: The six members of the Identification Committee will oversee the renewal of the Scientific Council for Horizon Europe 2028–2034.

title: EU and Jordan step up delivery of their strategic partnership through €135 million financing agreements
content: Today in Amman, Commissioner for the Mediterranean, Dubravka Šuica, has signed financing agreements worth €135 million with Jordan, alongside the country's Minister of Planning and International Cooperation, Zeina Toukan.
